canonical_reading_order: source and tests for the active task

product.goal: Turn multi-agent coding orchestration into a stable, inspectable, and recoverable execution kernel for OpenClaw-based development workflows.
non_goals: production-grade real worker execution engine before the contracts are ready
broad speculative abstraction without current MVP pressure
treating quality gates as planned task owners

invariants: needs_fix, blocked, and failed remain distinct
retry handoff preserves evidence and blocker metadata
logical model labels and exact model metadata stay aligned

module_map.src/planning: planning mode resolution, direct/debate flow, normalization, synthesis
module_map.src/orchestrator: dag build, runtime loop, dispatch, quality gates, retry, reporting
module_map.src/adapters: openclaw envelopes, model routing, exact-model resolution
module_map.src/workers: worker execution contracts and retry handoff
module_map.tests: adapter, planning, runtime, and persistence coverage

tasks.title: Add goose recipe assets and task-to-recipe mapping
Route implementation work through goose while keeping quality gates external
Implement PR6 human approval and orchestration controls
Implement PR7 budget, policy, and safety controls
Implement PR8 evaluation suite and golden scenarios
Implement PR9 CLI entry points and goose delivery workflow

workflow.pr_strategy: one task-sized PR at a time
workflow.merge_policy: local required verification must pass before merge
workflow.async_review_policy: review may finish later and follow-up can land in a later PR

notes: Use this artifact for fast repository orientation.
Re-read affected source files and tests before editing.
Do not treat this artifact as a substitute for current code.

known_gaps: goose is not yet wired into the implementation dispatcher
repo context automation is being bootstrapped
quality gates should remain orchestrator-owned during goose integration
